---
layout: docs
page_title: Pre-authorize sessions
description: How to pre-authorize Boundary sessions
---

# Pre-authorize sessions

In the standard workflow, Boundary performs authorization when you attempt to connect to a target.
However, you can pre-authorize a session and connect to it later using an authorization token.
Using an authorization token can save time, reduce API calls, and enable two-step or automated workflows.

Separating authorization from the connection process can additionally improve security.
Creating authorization tokens for your users in advance prevents them from having to know or manage connection information.

Complete the following steps to pre-authorize a session:

1. Log in to Boundary.
1. Use the following command and provide the target ID and host ID for the target you want to pre-authorize a session to:
   ```shell-session
   boundary targets authorize-session -id <TARGET_ID> -host-id <HOST_ID>
   ```

   Boundary issues an authorization token like the example below:

   ```shell-session
   Target information:
     Authorization Token:
     
     Created Time:          Tue, 7 Apr 2026 17:26:33 MST
     Endpoint:              tcp://127.0.0.1:16001
     Host ID:               hst_DHei2VpkBH
     Scope ID:              p_VF6GGqKxMz
     Session ID:            s_mraN5QJr7t
     Target ID:             ttcp_wtXnow8Krb
     Type:                  tcp
     User ID:               u_1234567890
    ```
1. Copy the authorization token.
1. When you are ready to connect to the target for which you pre-authorized a session, use the following command and provide the authorization token value:
  ```shell-session
  boundary connect -authz-token <AUTHZ_TOKEN>
  ```

  Boundary connects to the target and establishes the session you pre-authorized.

## More information

- To better understand sessions, refer to [Overview of sessions](/boundary/docs/targets/sessions).
- For more specific information about how the session resource relates to other Boundary resources, refer to the [Sessions](/boundary/docs/domain-model/sessions) domain model topic.
- To view the possible session parameters, refer to the session [CLI](/boundary/docs/commands/sessions) or [API](/boundary/api-docs/session-service) documentation.

## Next steps

- [View session information](/boundary/docs/targets/sessions/view-sessions) including the session ID, user who initiated the session, target name, start date, and session status.
- [Cancel a session](/boundary/docs/targets/sessions/cancel-sessions) if you no longer need it or detect unexpected activity.
